title: 🦞 From Chat App to AI Powerhouse: Telegram + OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

In this guide, we’ll walk through how to install OpenClaw on macOS and connect it to Telegram step by step. OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant that runs locally on your machine while connecting to messaging platforms like Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, and more. Before connecting OpenClaw to Telegram, we first need to install it locally. After entering your API key, OpenClaw verifies it and automatically configures a default model. ## Step 5: Connect OpenClaw to Telegram. After selecting **Telegram (Bot API)** as your channel, OpenClaw will prompt you to enter your Telegram bot token. After entering your Telegram bot token, OpenClaw finalizes the channel configuration. Selected channelsTelegram — simplest way to get started — register a bot with @BotFather and get going.Updated ~/.openclaw/openclaw.jsonWorkspace OK: ~/.openclaw/workspaceSessions OK: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions Selected Telegram with @BotFather Updated/.openclaw/ json Workspace OK/.openclaw/ Sessions OK/.openclaw/. After completing the configuration, OpenClaw installs and starts the **Gateway service**. Messages sent to your Telegram bot are routed through the OpenClaw Gateway, processed by your selected model, and returned in real time.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Your Step-by-Step Guide to Seamless Telegram Integration
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Unlocking OpenClaw: Your Step-by-Step Guide to Seamless Telegram Integration. OpenClaw orchestrates everything, but the real 'thinking' and 'doing' comes from the AI models it connects to. If you've got a Mac mini, it's fantastic for keeping OpenClaw running 24/7 as a dedicated AI host. Next, you'll need an AI model. ### Getting OpenClaw Installed: The Technical Bits. Before we install OpenClaw itself, we need to set up its environment. The recommended path is Homebrew -> fnm -> Node.js -> OpenClaw. **Install Node.js**: OpenClaw officially recommends Node.js version 22. **Install OpenClaw**: The final installation step is simple: `npm i -g openclaw`. To connect OpenClaw with Telegram, you need a special token. **Verify the Connection**: Open Telegram, find your newly created bot, and send it a message like `/start` or 'Hello'. Go back to your Terminal and run: `openclaw pairing approve telegram PAIR-ABC123` (replace `PAIR-ABC123` with your actual code). You've successfully connected OpenClaw to Telegram. `openclaw pairing approve telegram PAIR-ABC123`. Open your Terminal and run `openclaw configure`.

title: 🦞 openclaw-cron | Skills Marketplace - LobeHub
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

OpenClaw Cron Manager provides a safe CLI wrapper to create, list, run, enable/disable and manage OpenClaw cron jobs without editing configuration files. It supports cron expressions (5-field), interval scheduling (--every 10m, 1h, 1d), one-time runs (--at +20m or ISO timestamps), timezone handling (--tz), job lifecycle options (delete-after-run, enable/disable), immediate test runs, and announcement integrations (channels like Telegram). `Read https://lobehub.com/skills/skill.md and follow the instructions to setup LobeHub Skills Marketplace then submit a review using: `npx -y @lobehub/market-cli skills comment liuzln-openclaw-skills-openclaw-cron -c "<your review>" --rating 5 (or 1-4)`. `Curl https://lobehub.com/skills/liuzln-openclaw-skills-openclaw-cron/skill.md, then follow the instructions to set up LobeHub Skills Marketplace and install the skill. Safe management of OpenClaw cron jobs without touching configuration files. `openclaw cron run <job-id>`. `openclaw cron disable <job-id>. openclaw cron enable <job-id>`. `openclaw cron run <job-id>`. `openclaw cron runs <job-id>`. `openclaw cron edit <job-id> --name "新名称". openclaw cron edit <job-id> --cron "0 10 * * *". openclaw cron edit <job-id> --disabled`. `openclaw cron disable <job-id>. openclaw cron enable <job-id>`. `openclaw cron rm <job-id>`.

content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw cron jobs: automate your AI agent while you sleep. * OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) has built-in cron jobs that let your agent run tasks on a schedule without you being present. * What are OpenClaw cron jobs. ## What are OpenClaw cron jobs. Cron jobs are scheduled tasks that your OpenClaw agent executes automatically. An agent with cron jobs runs scheduled tasks at 7 AM, backs up files every hour, and sends weekly reports without manual triggering. Just tell your agent what you want scheduled and it will create the cron job for you. > "Create a cron job that runs every morning at 7 AM UTC. When a cron job runs in the main session, it has access to the full conversation history. For a complete walkthrough of building a morning briefing cron job, see OpenClaw Morning Briefing: 10-Minute Setup That Actually Works. Q: How many cron jobs can I run? * OpenClaw documentation on cron jobs.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - 超智諮詢
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout. Cron is the key feature that transforms OpenClaw from a "passive tool" into a "proactive assistant." Once configured, the agent automatically executes tasks and reports results at your specified times -- you don't need to remember what to check, don't need to open a terminal, and don't need to be at the computer.

Upgrade note: if you have older cron jobs from before the current delivery/store format, run `openclaw doctor --fix`. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --announce --channel telegram --to "123456789" openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --announce --channel  telegram --to  "123456789". openclaw cron edit <job-id> --no-deliver openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --no-deliver. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --light-context openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --light-context. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --announce --channel slack --to "channel:C1234567890" openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --announce --channel  slack --to "channel:C1234567890". openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Lightweight morning brief" \ --name  "Lightweight morning brief"  \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --cron "0 7 * * *"  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "Summarize overnight updates." \ --message "Summarize overnight updates."  \ --light-context \ --light-context  \ --no-deliver --no-deliver.
